case.title: Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
case.protocol: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).
case.notes: Model-matrix benchmark for upstream/council combinations on the DES isoxazole protocol.

pre_council_proposal.chemistry_notes: The reaction is a 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition involving a hazardous nitrile oxide intermediate. Flow chemistry is used here to minimize the holdup of this intermediate. The solvent is a viscous Deep Eutectic Solvent (DES), which explains the relatively low flow rate (0.5 mL/min) and the need for a BPR to prevent degassing at 120°C. The Damkohler number (Da = 1.97) suggests that mixing is becoming significant, so a T-mixer is used to ensure homogeneity before the reactor coil. The residence time is set to 15 min to match the batch reaction time, as the intensification factor (IF) from analogies was floored to 1.0x for safety.

case.title: Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
case.protocol: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).
case.notes: Model-matrix benchmark for upstream/council combinations on the DES isoxazole protocol.

analogies.summary: This process involves the synthesis of 4-methyl-1,2,5-oxadiazole, a highly energetic compound, using a nitration or cyclization reaction in a concentrated sulfuric acid/water mixture (63:6.3 ratio). The flow process was conducted in a tubular reactor at 154°C with a residence time of 179 minutes (approximately 3 hours) and a flow rate of 0.12 mL/min, requiring a back-pressure regulator at 47.6 bar to maintain liquid phase conditions. Flow chemistry enabled a significant temperature increase from 65°C in batch to 154°C, resulting in an improved yield from 70.8% to 78.8%. The primary advantage of flow over batch was enhanced safety when handling this highly energetic compound, as the continuous process allows for better control of the exothermic reaction and reduces the inventory of hazardous material at any given time. The high pressure and temperature conditions achievable in the flow reactor also contributed to the improved reaction efficiency.

The reaction uses DMSO as solvent with diisopropylamine as base, operating at 60°C with a 20-minute residence time at 0.165 mL/min flow rate, achieving 96% yield. The flow process allows for higher temperature operation (60°C vs 20°C in batch) while maintaining excellent yield, with the batch baseline achieving 100% yield at room temperature in the same reaction time. analogies.summary: This photocycloaddition reaction utilizes triplet sensitization with 2,2'-dimethoxythioxanthone (2,2'-MeOTX) as the photocatalyst at 1 mol% loading under 455 nm COB LED irradiation. The flow process operates in acetonitrile at 200°C with a 5-minute residence time and 6.0 mL/min flow rate using a FlowSyn steel coil reactor (30 mL volume) equipped with a 34 bar back-pressure regulator. Flow chemistry enabled daisy-chaining of the initial photochemical step with high-temperature thermal cascade reactions, dramatically improving yield from 16% in batch (2 hours) to 88% in flow with a 24-fold intensification factor. The high-temperature operation required for the thermal cascade was much safer to implement in the continuous flow reactor compared to batch processing.

Flow chemistry is used here to minimize the holdup of this intermediate. The solvent is a viscous Deep Eutectic Solvent (DES), which explains the relatively low flow rate (0.5 mL/min) and the need for a BPR to prevent degassing at 120°C. The Damkohler number (Da = 1.97) suggests that mixing is becoming significant, so a T-mixer is used to ensure homogeneity before the reactor coil.
